notes: Junagarh. The Bhavnath Mahadev Fair is held at the Bhavnath Mahadev Temple, situated at the foot of Mount Girnar in the city of Junagadh (Gujarat). A five day fair is held during the festival of Mahashivratri which is a worldwide tourist attraction. Other fairs celebrated in Gujarat: Chitra-Vichitra Fair, Dangs Durbar Fair, Shamlaji Fair, and Tarnetar Fair (Trinetrashwar Mahadev Fair).

Gujarat. Ambaji Bhadarvi Poonam Fair takes place in the month of Bhadarvo Ambaji, Gujarat. The fair is organized by the Shri Arasuri Ambaji Mata Devasthan Trust and the Banaskantha District Administration. The fair is considered a major religious gathering in western India, with over 25 lakh devotees visiting the temple town. Other Fair of Gujarat - Tarnetar Fair, Kavant Tribal Fair, Chitra Vichitra Tribal Fair, Madhavrai Fair.
five. Fair and Festivals of Gujarat: Rann Utsav, International Kite Festival, Tarnetar fair, Megh Malhar Parv Saputara, Vibrant Navratri Festival, Dang Darbar Fair, Kavant Fair.

Karnataka. Kadalekayi Parishe is an annual groundnut fair held in Basavanagudi, Bangalore, at the Bull Temple, usually in November or December. Dedicated to Lord Ganesha, it marks the harvest season for groundnuts. The fair features prayers, cultural events, and local markets where farmers sell their fresh produce. It is a centuries-old tradition celebrating Karnataka's agricultural heritage.

West Bengal. Poush Mela is an annual fair and festival that takes place in Santiniketan, in Birbhum District, marking the harvest season. Other Fair of West Bengal - Kenduli Mela, Bhim Mela, Rash Mela, Ganga Sagar Mela, Jalpesh Mela, Kolkata Book Fair.

Rajasthan. Other fairs and festivals in Rajasthan: Camel Festival, Mahaveerji Fair, Baneshwar Fair , Kolayat Fair, Marwar Festival, and Dhulandi Festival. Fairs and Festivals of India: Tripura - Poush Sankranti, Garia, and Kharchi. Punjab - Chhappar Mela, Lohri, Hola Mohalla, and Baisakhi. Maharashtra - Banganga Festival, and Pola Festival.

Uttar Pradesh. Sardhana Christian Fair : Every year on the 1st Sunday of the month of November a large feast is held in honor of Mother Mary. Other Fairs of Uttar Pradesh : Kumbh Mela (the festival of the sacred Pitcher), Gau Charan Fair, Nauchandi Fair, Dewa Sharif Fair, Shakumbhari Fair, Magh Fair (Prayagraj), Rath Fair (Vrindavan), Agra Cattle Fair or Bateshwar Fair (Agra).
Assam. Ambubachi Mela is an annual four-day Hindu mela held at Kamakhya Temple in Guwahati, Assam. It symbolizes the fertility cult of Goddess Kamakhya. Other Fair of Assam - Jonbeel Mela.

Jammu. Bahu Mela, one of the largest Hindu festivals in Jammu, is a vibrant celebration held at Bahu Fort. Major festivals in Jammu and Kashmir include the Tulip Festival, Shikara Festival, Saffron Festival and Gurez Festival.

Gujarat. Dangs Darbar is a fair held during the Holi festival in Ahwa district in Gujarat. Traditionally, tribal people perform dance and music, celebrating it as a festival. Notable festivals in Gujarat include Navratri, Rann Utsav, International Kite Festival (Uttarayan), Modhera Dance Festival, Makar Sankranti, Janmashtami, and Diwali.

Surajkund Mela (Faridabad). This is one of the most celebrated international Craftsmela and is organised by the Haryana Tourism Department. Shamlaji fair (Gujarat). The Kapil Muni Fair is the largest fair of Bikaner (Rajasthan). The Chitra Vichitra Fair (Gujarat).

Alwar (Rajasthan). The Shri Jagannathji Mela starts with a procession of Lord Jagannath passing through the city. Other Fairs of Rajasthan: Karni Mata Mela (Bikaner); Beneshwar Mela (Dungarpur) and Pushkar Fair (Ajmer).

Pushkar (Rajasthan) camel fair held each November at the time of the Kartik Purnima. Fairs/Festivals of Rajasthan : Bikaner camel festival is celebrated in the month of January. Barmer Cattle Fair is held every year in the months of March and April. The Mewar festival Udaipur, is held each year in March-April to welcome spring. The Bundi Festival is celebrated in the month of Kartik (October-November).

Uttar Pradesh. This fair starts on the day of Makar Sankranti. This fair is held every 12 years, with the location of the observance rotating among four pilgrimage sites on the four sacred rivers: Ganga at Haridwar, Triveni Sangam at Prayagraj, the Godavari at Nashik, and Shipra at Ujjain. Ardha (half) Kumbh Mela is held at only two places, Haridwar and Allahabad, every sixth year and a Maha Kumbh is held after every years. It is the 13th intangible cultural heritage from India to be listed on UNESCO's list.
Kshipra river. Ujjain Simhastha is a Hindu religious mela held every 12 years in the Ujjain city of Madhya Pradesh.

Both a and b. Sonepur Cattle Fair is one of Asia's largest cattle fairs, organized at the confluence of two rivers, the Ganga and Gandak in Bihar. Nagaji Fair is celebrated in the tribal area of Madhya Pradesh. Q.709. Sheorinarayan fair is held in which of the following states? SSC CHSL 17/08/2023 (4th shift) (a) West Bengal (b) Jharkhand (c) Chhattisgarh (d) Madhya Pradesh Sol.709.(c) Chhattisgarh. The fair is held every year in the month of February during the Magh Purnima. The Sherorinarayan Temple which is devoted to Lord Rama is the main venue of this Fair.

West Bengal. Gangasagar fair is held annually on the occasion of Makar Sankranti (January) on Sagar Island's southern tip, where the Ganges river enters the Bay of Bengal. This confluence is Gangasagar. Fairs of West Bengal: Bera Utsav, Orange Festival, Rath Yatra Festival, Jalpesh Festival, Ramkeli Mela, Poush Mela, Raas Mela, Book Fair & Film Festival, etc. Q.711. The Champaran Mela is celebrated every year in which city of Chhattisgarh? Graduate Level 05/08/2022 ( Shift - 3) (a) Bilaspur (b) Raipur (c) Raigarh (d) Korna Sol.711.(b) Raipur. The Annual Fair of Champaran is held with great festivities in the month of Magh every year.

Nalwari Fair. Doongri Festival also known as Hidimba Devi Fair is celebrated in Kullu on the occasion of Basant Panchami. Ashwin Navratri Mela is celebrated in Himachal Pradesh and Ashwin Amavasya Mela is celebrated in Uttar Pradesh.
